w:r.w:t: Partner Center

How to check if all the connections are working properly?
Important checklist to follow
Campaign production checklist:
h
ttps://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1CHyhigFSE62jVBvuNClVoF89iNQLhmcUJAXsTaTSyqw/edit#gid=1167792690
Ad Creation Template:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1CHyhigFSE62jVBvuNClVoF89iNQLhmcUJAXsTaTSyqw/edit#gid=1383358881
Existing Ad Copies for Reference:
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1CHyhigFSE62jVBvuNClVoF89iNQLhmcUJAXsTaTSyqw/edit#gid=651893632
Optimization Checklist
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1CHyhigFSE62jVBvuNClVoF89iNQLhmcUJAXsTaTSyqw/edit#gid=1920966515
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1CHyhigFSE62jVBvuNClVoF89iNQLhmcUJAXsTaTSyqw/edit#gid=651893632
Campaign Optimization Template
VMKA Tasks & Process Flow
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1oZi6KOhhfks0o0CkKxXWvCQLC3to2yh6H0kNyA1P8yc/edit
Dashboards
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1oWd6FfrytCr1zv7uRQxI0fdrX5gVjEYaJq1k6B6Ady8/edit
Timesheet
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ALG2qkRZGJGwx3-b53kTfgz0Zq62J_Yr7dUPqt_PqBU/edit#gid=0
Landing page and Creatives Requirement
Landing page Checklist
GA4 training
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1IQu8pdO5OR0FpYJO0ehH8JSQQlKhJHrEVQF-A3Dk5y8/edit#
Campaign Best Practices

WDUjR5R6Rgs
/edit
Conversion Rates of Currencies
Subscriptions have CAD at 1.25 of USD, and AUD at 0.98 from CAD;
Marketing Services products have both CAD/AUD at 1.25 of USD;
All other O&O and 3rd Party Vendor products have CAD/AUD at 1.4 of USD;
Snapshots/Refreshes - USD ($2), CAD ($3), AUD ($4) for new subscription tiers, but still $2 for all currencies on FREE (to be updated to MSRP shortly)
Partner Center
Go to >>
https://partners.vendasta.com/
Keep the mouse cursor inside the dashboard and press backslash (/) to switch between partners.
Click switch partners
Place the PID you want to check
On the right-hand side, you can view the following:
Check the accounts under Business >> Put the correct account name and click on the name of the account.
On the above screenshot you will be able to see the active products.
Go to administration >> Billing >>
Click on my billing >> By account >> Remove the start and end date.
Search for the account you want to >> Click on the numbers as highlighted
You can see the paid product details. This will be helpful to see whether we are receiving payments on a monthly basis.
Google Ad
Production
Process
Create a new account as per the instructions provided (
MC Google Connect
Update the structure of the campaign
Specialists Create the
adcopies
on the MC platform
Add relevant, targeted keywords, and negative keywords to the campaign
Specialists assign the ticket to the QA team to review the campaign's whole setup along with ads & keywords.
The QA team rechecks the
adcopies
and makes the changes if required.
QA confirms the Specialist about the changes made and sends the downloadable Excel sheet (
template)
to the comms team for approval.
Once approval is received, specialists recheck the copies and keywords and make the campaign live.
Note: If there are any changes to the copy or keywords specialists make the edits and re-assign the QA team to recheck the edits.
Once confirmation is received from the QA team, specialists make the campaign live and update the ticket & VTM.
MC Google Connect Account Creation Screenshots
Select Appropriate instance based on the partner paying currency & business location
Click
+New Account
to create a new ad account for a specific SMB
Update Account Name (SMB Name), Xref ID (AGID) & Select the market (Vendasta) to initiate the account setup
Updating required informations about the SMB & internal team to complete the account creation
Under Segments, Click actions and select either
Add or Link Existing
to start creating a campaign.
Add
- If you are going to setup a campaign from scratch with MC Connect (Recommended)
Link Existing
If you already created an account and campaign directly in Google Ads and just need to link with MC to manage the budget & automated bidding
Provide a name to your campaign and select the campaign type
Create or link a Google ad account
Create Google Ads Account
- If we don’t have any existing ad account for the SMB (Recommended)
Link Existing Google Ads Account
- If we have an existing ad account for the SMB.
Update the Google ad account information along with Bing and then click OK.
Select Bing ads & provide Suffix for Bing Segment Names.
Make sure we have successfully created the Google ad account
Add a final url for the segment and click OK
Add the required assets based on the information available. Call Assets, Sitelinks, Callout & Structured Snippets are the highly recommended assets to update.

Geographic Targets, Exclusions, Geomodifiers, Advanced Location Targeting, Audience Targets & Exclusion (Not preferred for search ads), Demographics, Ad Schedule ( If partner shared any conditions), and Negative Keyphrases
Create an Ad Group
add from category
- We can create an ad group instantly by selecting from a set of predefined ad group data related to your industry. We already have a predefined ad group assets for all industries
add empty ad group
- If you want to add unique target keywords and ad copy based on your own analysis, you can choose this option.
Name the ad group and select No to keep the ad group active
Add RSA ads, Call ads, Positive Keyphrases & Negative Keyphrases to complete the campaign build
Add 10 Headlines & 4 descriptions for a RSA ads
Add business name, Phone number, 2 headlines and 2 descriptions along with the Verification URL to create a call ad
Add positive keywords to target from your keyword analysis (Broad, Phrase & Exact)
Add negative keywords in the ad group level to avoid irrelevant clicks to the campaign. (Broad, Phrase & Exact)
Back to campaign level and add the budget cycles to set the campaign active. This can be done after the approval received from the partner.
We can add 12 budget cycles with a monthly target ad spend provided by the partner
Here is the final budget cycle. Once we added the budget cycle and set all segments & ad groups active, the campaign will start performing
Three Major
Benefits of Google Connect Setup:
Easy customization available (Adding segments, ad groups, ads, location target) and lots more

An AdVantage Connect account, unlike a Standard account, can be edited directly in the channel (e.g., Google Ads UI) or a third-party editor (e.g., Google Ads Editor) while still being linked to and managed by AdVantage.
In a Standard account, campaigns and segments can only be edited within the AdVantage UI or via the ROAR API. This is a one-way relationship, where AdVantage is considered the source of truth.
Benefits of Using Google Connect
Existing Benefits for AdVantage vs. Google
Google Connect continues to offer AdVantage's many reseller-oriented features that cannot be found in Google, including:
Taxonomies for search advertising
Sales-oriented estimator
Flexible budget-cycle support with reporting in retail amounts
Google Analytics integration with easy setup and automated uploads of Google Analytics conversions to Google Ads
Notifications (diagnostics and disapprovals)
User role management
Localized merchant reporting
New Benefits for Connect Accounts

Connect accounts can use the Google Ads UI to access targeting or detailed Google Ads settings that are not supported via the AdVantage UI or API (like image extensions or Google Ads auto-applied recommendations)
Connect accounts can use Google Ads "beta" features that are not supported in the Google Ads API (features that are not supported in the Google Ads API cannot be built into AdVantage)
Connect accounts can use non-MatchCraft tools to edit (e.g. Google Ads Editors)
Support for Google Video campaigns (this is a reporting-only segment type due to Google limitations that prevent creating and/or editing Google Video campaigns in the Google Ads API)
The ability to link individual Google Ads accounts to AdVantage (instead of using the Wasabi migration process)
Future Benefits Coming to Connect Accounts
The ability to create segments using other Google campaign types (like Google Display, Google Shopping, Google Performance Max, etc.)
The ability to mix Google Ads with other channels like Facebook, Bing, and Yelp
MC Installation Steps
Step1
Click New Account on the left side of the screen.
Provide the proper Campaign Name (This will be helpful to make the reviews in the future without any hassle.
Select the market:
Vendasta (USA)
Vendasta CA (Canada)
Vendasta AU (Australia)
Vendasta UK (United Kingdom)
Click “OK”
Step2:
Fill in the required information in the second stage.
Account Name:
AGID ID (The most important one to enter)
Labels (Used for future reference)
Sales Rep (Can select your TL name or Manager name)
Campaign Manager (Can be anyone (Maybe yours too)
Skip Link Estimate
Step3:
Type Proper Account name: Name of the Account_Source for Example Absolute Solutions_Search
Create Account Budget
Step4:
Enter Ad Segment Name (Accountname_Type of Campaign)
Type of the campaign:
Final URL used for the campaign
Step5
Under the Segment Info you can see the information properly.
Step6:
Select the ad groups and start creating ad groups
Add ad groups as per the campaign structure with proper naming convention(This will help other specialists in future optimizing and reporting.
Step7
Upload the responsive ads using the add button
Step8
Place to add finally approved keywords. At an earlier stage keywords are added on phrase match, and based on the performance this can be changed either to phrase or exact match.
Step9:
Locations Target
Add the locations under Geographic targets. And add the locations which need to be excluded under Geographic Exclusions.
Step10:
Clicks the Extensions: Add the sitelink extensions, Call out extensions and structured Snippets.
Always test conversions using the sample GA Accounts before using the actual GA account using the below method.
Test Conversions using Sample GA Account:
Create a Tag & Variable on GTM as Test GA
Setup the conversions want to track
Use the landing page and test conversions.
Check the real time data of analytics
Check under conversions if its firing properly
If an event has been set up, check the events under Realtime.
Google Analytics and GTM Integration
Create a Filtered View on analytics
Blocking internal IPs can be done through this account
Add the Website URL and Select the Create Universal Analytics properly only (In future we may go with GA4 analytics)
Basic setups required like Business size, what type of measurements and create a view.
Once you create you will be able to get the tracking code information, take the UA Code and keep it ready.
Go to GTM (google tag manager) -
https://marketingplatform.google.com/about/tag-manager
Go to variables and click new under User-Defined Variables . Add the copied UA code in the variable section and save. Name the variable, which is easy to remember.
Under Tag click new tag. Click Tag type >> Select Google Analytics: Universal Analytics
Select the track type to Page View
Select the Analytics Settings (Saved Variable)
Select Triggering >> All pages (This tag should cover and track all the pages of the website)
Click save >> Publish Workspace
Note: If the Publish button is not done then the GA Tracking will not work.
After Publish is done go to
Analytics and
check if the real time is working.
Remember to check a few pages on the website to check whether all pages have been tracked properly after GTM integration.
Goals
Tracking Without Thankyou page (Same page thank you message)
Go to the landing page of the website
Fix the button you want to track conversions
GTM Setup
Go to GTM >> Click >> Variables
Switch on: Click the built in variables >> Configure >> Click all the elements under click and forms.
Fix the button you want to track conversions
New Tag >> Track Type Event
Under category >> Provide a name, which you can identify easier
Similar to the category name of the Action can be provided with proper name, which can be recognised soon
Select the analytics setting

Triggering >> Click (+) - Element Visibility >> CSS Selector >> Select the entire thank you message >> right click >> Inspect >> Select the code >> right click & Copy >> Copy Selector and update on the GTM.
Switch on Observe DOM changes
Goals Tracking With Thank you page
GTM Setup:
Click Tag >> Add the category and Action name
Select the Analytics account
Tigger : Page view >> Some Page Views >> Page URL >> Thankyou URL
Save the tag and publish the workspace.
Note: Verify if there are multiple forms and each form has a different thank you page.
Bing Ads Setup
At the Account level under the “Details” tab and within the “Campaign Info” settings, we should click on Edit & select “Bing” under “Engines” and Click “OK”.
Then navigate to the Segment level by clicking on any of the Segments.
“Geographic Targets” need to be selected for “Bing” Engines.
If the location is specified by radius, MC will have both Google & Bing selected by default.
If the location is specified by the engine code, we must select the engine (Google or Bing).
Type in the Area name and search for it and select the Bing Engine code and add them to the Location Target.
These settings need to be replicated in other Segments as well under the same Campaign.

We are enabling the “Search Partner Network” for Google Engine if we are facing any Pacing issues. Likewise, we need to turn on the “Syndicated Search Partner Network” for Bing if there are any Pacing issues.
At the Segment level and under “Network Targeting” edit and select the “Additional Bing Network Target:”.
Connecting Bing On Ad Intel:
Under the “Settings” tab, Select “Connections”
Select “Connect” in “Microsoft Ads”
Once you log in to the Bing Advertising Account, Select the “Microsoft Ads Account”.
Search for the Account & Select the Account & Connect it.
Bing Ads Conversion Tracking
Login
https://ads.microsoft.com/cc/welcome/index
Select the account you want to add UET Tags
Under conversion tracking click >> UET Tags
Click Create >> Add UET tag name >> UET tag description
Enable Microsoft Clarity (This will help us to see users interaction and heatmaps)
Install the code using Tag Manager
GTM Integration with Bing Analytics
Click New Tag >> Search for Microsoft Advertising Universal Event tracking
Add the UET Tag picked from Bing Conversions
Trigger >> All pages
Save and Publish
Creating a Bing Ads Tracking Conversion Goal - thank you page URL
To start creating goals, navigate to the left side of the screen, and click
Conversion Tracking
. Under
Conversion Tracking
, find and click
Conversion goals
Click the
Create conversion goal
button.
Screenshot of button to click for conversion goal creation
A new screen will come up. In the box labeled
Goal name
, type in a goal name that describes your goal. The name you choose should make it easy to identify if you have several.
The next step is to select the type of conversion you want to track.
What type of goal do you want to set?
Choose the best fit, and click
Next
Conversion Goal Type: Purchase
Select the type of interaction you would like to track with this goal.
Conversion Goal Type: Destination URL

When you use this goal, you track when someone visits a certain number of pages. It’s also greater than the goal, so you’ll set it for 1 page less than you would like your goal to be. Place the number you choose in the box under Pages per visit.
To set up your event goal, you’ll need to fill in the boxes for
Category
Action
Label
, and
Value
Click Next >>
Set up tagging
Creating a Bing Ads Tracking Conversion Goal - Event
Select the goal you want to track >> Select event
Create an Event goal with action and revenue
Go to next >> Select the option you want to track
Track event on inline action
Tract event on page load

On each page where this event happens (for example, your order confirmation page or purchase completion page), copy this tag and paste it in between the <head> and </head> tags right after the base UET tag.
Marchex Installation
Marchex
Call Tracking, Call Analytics, Text Messaging & SMS for Businesses. AI-Powered Call & Text software for Sales & Marketing Teams.
Step1:
Go to
https://www.marchex.io/
Click account, and Under account, click Order toll Free numbers.
Step2:
Based on the instance (Location) select the area code.
Click next
Step3
: Confirm new order
Step4:
Selected number will be shown here.
Step5:
Go to Client list >> Click New Client
Add the company name
Address and other informations related to the business
Step6:
Setup s user for this client now >> Select yes
Add the number purchased under inbound number
Forward to number: This should be taken from the client website or asked from the partner.
Click “
Next” and Done.
Step7:
Under Campaign settings add the following details
Add the ad name and check all the settings before you click save
Step8:
Click Advanced from the account settings.
Select Javascript rewrite
Select the campaign you wanted to do changes
Add the domain name as search calls (Source of conversion tracking is possible from this setting)
Step8:
Copy the code and place it on the website before the </body> tag. This step is very important to track the calls from the campaign
Marchex GTM integration
To avoid too many code installations on the website, Marchex can be easily integrated into GTM.
Click Tags
Select Custom HTML
Add the copied Marchex code into the HTML section
Click the Triggering section >> Select all pages
Select the
DOM ready
under the triggering section.
DOM Ready (
used
to make a function available after the document is loaded
Step2:
Select Save and publish Workspace
Ad Intel Installation
Load the below URL
Change the Partners PID and AGID alone to load the corresponding partner
Click the connection under settings
Select the Connect under Google ads
https://www.advertisingintelligence.io/partner/TPMT/business/AG-GW8Q7PZ7JP/overview
Step2:
Authorize the account to select the Google ads >> Click allow
Select the id appropriate adwords account and click connect account
Step 3:
Google Analytics Integration
For connecting Google Analytics accounts. Click connect from analytics section
Step 4:
Marchex Integration
If a partner ordered Marchex, it's required to connect Marchex with Ad Intelligence.
Similarly the Facebook and Microsoft connections are done by selecting the appropriate account.
How to check if all the connections are working properly?
Go to overview to check the overall performance
For individual performance click the analytics, Phone calls, facebook ads or Microsoft ads.
Dynamic Auto Ads Process Guide
Important
Ensure MotoMiner Reports are generated and shared with partners every month.
Order the Catalog asap!
Customers must get the retargeting code installed on their site for their dynamic auto ads to function properly.
Never mention the existence of MotoMiner in any partner facing communications or vtm notes.

Meta will consistently flag these ads as special ad category “credit”. So long as we do not mention financing or auto loans in our ad copy this flag is incorrect. Do not accept this flag appeal and fight it with Meta every time it happens. If Meta rejects the appeal replace the ad with a new one with new ad copy and if rejected apply for appeal again.
Appeal manually through live chat. Do not click the request review button.
Ordering a Catalog
When an order for Dyn Auto Ads first comes in we need to order a catalog from MotoMiner asap to ensure we are meeting turnaround times.
This can be done by filling out the below template and sending it to (
vendastasupport@zerosum.ai

Note: You will need to create an ad account so you can grab its ad account ID and Pixel ID to share in the email. You do also need to share the pixel ID to their BM as mentioned at the end of the email template.
Once Motominer sends us an email confirming the catalog is ready we can proceed with production.
Email Template
We would like a catalog created for
(Dealership Name)
within the
(Partner Name)
Division. We would also like the additional lookalike audiences added to Ad Account
[ID #]
. Please let me know if you require any additional information.
URL For Dealership Website:
(Dealership URL)
This catalog is for the Business Manager:
Digital Marketing Services
Business Manager ID: 865125153846998
And the Pixel ID:
(Pixel ID)
as well as the above-mentioned ad account has been shared with your business manager. (BM ID: 259373031180444)
Auto Dealer Dynamic Ad Pixel Placement
Same as we ask partners to install the GTM code on their site for Dyn Auto Ads we also have to ask that they get a special retargeting code installed.
Overview

When you include this JavaScript code on your website, it will begin to tag shoppers with the specific vehicle(s) they look at, so they can be retargeted later when they access a website or service where Facebook provides ads. Our tracking code also tags shoppers with vehicle types they are interested in, so if they access a Ford F150 truck, for instance, we will tag them as interested in "Trucks."
Include this code as high in the <head> of the website as possible:
<!-- MotoMiner GTM -->

<script>(function(w,d,s,l,i){w[l]=w[l]||[];w[l].push({'gtm.start':new Date().getTime(),event:'gtm.js'});var f=d.getElementsByTagName(s)[0],j=d.createElement(s),dl=l!='dataLayer'?'&l='+l:'';j.async=true;j.src='https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtm.js?id='+i+dl;f.parentNode.insertBefore(j,f);})(window,document,'script','dataLayer','GTM-NFTX3XB');</script>
<!-- End MotoMiner GTM →
Additionally, include this code immediately after the opening <body> tag:
<!-- MotoMiner GTM (
noscript
noscript
><iframe src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/ns.html?id=GTM-NFTX3XB" height="0" width="0" style="display:none;visibility:hidden"></iframe></noscript>
<!-- End MotoMiner GTM (
noscript
Dyn Auto Ads Campaign Build
For every Dyn Auto Ads we will be creating two campaigns:
A traffic campaign optimizing for link clicks
A retargeting campaign optimizing for content views.
The Link Click campaign should have four ad sets
General - NEW
Typical General audience
Ads will feature new vehicles
Example targeting below (update and adjust to match the types of vehicle offered by the dealership).
General - USED
Same ad set setup as the “General - NEW”
Ads will feature used vehicles
Vehicle LAA - NEW
Created using custom audiences provided to us by Motominer.
As this is a lookalike, interest targeting should be removed.
Add in all custom audiences provided to us by Motominer (these should show up after clicking into the search bar for custom audiences.
Example below (note: name of audiences may not always match)
Vehicle LAA - USED
Same ad set setup as the “Vehicle LAA - NEW”
Ads will feature used vehicles
Every ad set should have at least two ad versions (different ad copies) but should scale up with budget as appropriate.
USED ad sets ads can use the same ad copy as each other. Same for NEW ad sets ads.
All ads are built as carousel ads but we have to select “Catalog” as the creative source.
Select the correct catalog under Ad creative (should be some variant of the name of the dealership. Motominer will tell us the name in their email response to the catalog order.
For NEW ad sets select “All-New” as the vehicle set, for USED ad sets select “All-Used” as the vehicle set.
Note: In some cases the partner may specifically request to have ads only promoting certain brands or other categories, you can browse the vehicle set options in these cases to see what we can offer.
Since these ads are Dynamic once we’ve selected the Catalog and the Vehicle Set the creative will self generate.
Note: On a new build this may take a few hours. Do not be alarmed if the catalog is showing as empty unless this is still the case for several hours.

For our Headline and Description we should be using dynamic text with the vehicle title in the headline and vehicle price in the description. You can select these by pressing the “+” icon next to the field.

For the ad copy we can usually start out by using several of the ones we’ve found effective in the past however we need to make sure these match with the dealership, the season and whether it’s a Used or New ad set.
Note: I’ve included some that we use in several campaigns below feel free to browse other dyn auto ad accounts for more examples.
Find your dream vehicle from our great selection below.
With our high-quality vehicles and ever-changing inventory, we're sure you'll find a vehicle you love!
Ready to start your search for your next vehicle?
We're always adding great new & used vehicles to our inventory.
See what's available today!
Don't stop your search for the perfect vehicle!
At (Dealership), we bring in the best vehicles to suit all of your needs!
Keep searching for the perfect vehicle that meets all your unique needs! 🚙
Browse through our great selection of affordable pre-owned vehicles!
The retargeting campaign will need to have its campaign objective set to “Catalog Sales' ' and then select the catalog below that.
This campaign will have just one retargeting ad set with all vehicles selected as its vehicle set.

No interest targeting just demographic and geographic targeting. Then select “Retarget ads to people who interacted with your products on and off Facebook” and set every options date range to 0 - 60 days.
Finally ensure we have the optimization for ad delivery set to “Convesion event” and event type “view content”.
Ads are setup the same as for the link click campaign, just make sure since we are using all vehicles that the ad copy does not say these are only new or only used vehicles.
Launching Dyn Auto
Mostly a standard launch, remember about 50% MGMT fee to ensure your budget is set correctly when connecting to MatchCraft.
Start with a budget split of 90% traffic campaign / 10% retargeting campaign, can update this month to month during optimizations depending on performance.
No pixel setup should be needed as Motominer should have done all this but double check that all events are created.
Optimizing Dynamic Auto Ads
Optimizations for Dyn Auto Ads are generally fairly simple since so much is automated.
Generally if the campaign is brining in clicks for less then a $0.60 CPC the campaign is doing well. Our goal is to drive a large volume of traffic to their site and to view their vehicles.
Here is a list of common potential optimizations we can make to Dyn Auto Ad.
Increase an ad sets min ad spend if it’s doing well but not spending much.
Shift budget between the retargeting and traffic campaign based on which is preforming better.
Introduce a new ad copy for split testing.
Note: This should be done at least once every 3 months, assign to copywriter or use a successful ad copy from another dyn auto campaign.
Introduce a new lookalike audience for split testing to the traffic campaign.
The only changes we can make to the ads creative is selecting a different vehicle set but should only do this when instructed.
Generating Monthly Reports
Within the first 5 days of the month we need to generate and send out the Motominer reports to the partner for all their Dyn Auto Ads clients.
Assign this as a monthly recurring task in vtm at the start of the month.
Quick summary of the steps required:
Create a vtm task for yourself on the first business day of every month for any dynamic auto campaign you manage.
Log in to Motominer https://motominer.com/ using credentials in the lifeline.
Generate a report for the full previous calendar month not the billing cycle
Save file name as "Dealership Name - Month Year" DO NOT leave Motominer in the file name.
Upload the file to the account on partner center.
In vtm task attach the pdf file as well and assign the vtm tag to the comms team member so they can email the report to the partner as well.
Create next month's task for the first business day of that month (for future dyn auto campaign's we will make this part of the template).
Training Video:
https://www.loom.com/share/b8154c632b6e4d8b978879a32cecdb1f
MAN Marketing Accounts
For The Man Marketing, we have the following list of active accounts.
60 to Escape (WI)
Coffman Truck Sales
Curtis Cleaners
Curtis Equipment Co.
Audibel
Subaru Orland Park
McGrath Lexus of Chicago
McGrath Lexus Westmont
The Beyond Shades was canceled this month.
Working Process -

For all the above-mentioned accounts we need to monitor the campaigns and perform a weekly pacing check in order to maintain the spending. If any of the campaigns is under pacing, we need to flag it with the Comm’s person
Nami Dave
during our weekly call.
We used to have a bi-weekly call with the OLQL partners for discussing the performance and pacing status of all the campaigns. Specialists are not required to join this call.
The PDM (Jer) will be updating the Ad spend on the
MAN Marketing Sheet
(End of every Month) once they receive confirmation from the partner.
Once the Budget is confirmed, We need to make sure that we have added the future budget cycles for all the campaigns with the approved Ad spend.

Regarding the Monthly Optimization, We will be providing the recommendations to the partner and once it is approved only we should be actioning them on the platform(Google - Search/Display). The approval will be sent over the mail (Zendesk) to the comm’s person.
We should follow the monthly date range for doing optimizations since the campaigns follow the monthly budget cycle (1st to 30th or 31st).
All the Optimizations should be completed before the 3rd of every Month. So the Comm’s person can work on the Tapclicks documents and it will be sent to the partner by the 5th of every month.

For some of the Display campaigns, we will be receiving monthly promos from the partner. The status of the promo’s will be updated in the Display + Youtube Creatives Tab in the Man Marketing sheet. It will be given with a start date and Expiry date or if it's an Evergreen creative. Please reach out to the Comm’s person if you have any doubts.
MAN Marketing sheet -
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QDHVs9spLKa6BJHGqItidKyrvwlJXCX2kQ395xrWKpc/edit#gid=1792279698
Primary Contact Person - Dinesh
Comm’s Person -
Nami Dave
PDM - Jer Basset
GTM Installation
Wordpress - Simple Method
Log into your WordPress admin panel.
Go to Plugins / Add new.
Search for “Google Tag Manager.”
Click on “Install now” in the search results next to “DuracellTomi’s Google Tag Manager for WordPress.”
Now, go to Settings / Google Tag Manager and enter your GTM container ID.
Other Method:
Go to Themed Editor >> header.php
Here you will find the head tag and the body tag. Let’s copy the first part of the code and place it beneath the opening head tag.
Once installed go to the Website source code >> Right click >> View source code >> Check if the code is installed properly on the website.
Note: Please take the backup of the code before you paste the GTM script
Shopify Integration: (Method 1)
Copy your Universal Google Analytics (GA) script from Google Analytics > Property > Tracking Info > Tracking Code
Paste your Google Analytics script into your Shopify Admin > Sales Channels > Online Store > Google Analytics
Copy your Google Tag Manager snippet from your GTM admin
(NOTE: without the opening and closing <script> </script> tags):
Paste the copied script in your Shopify Account > Sales Channels > Online Store > Google Analytics > Additional Google Analytics Javascript
Shopify Integration: (Method 2)
Copy your GTM script into your theme.liquid at top of <head>
If you have a developer or agency then ensure you are requesting this to be put near the top of your opening <head>
Into this:
Step2:
Copy your GTM
noscript
into your theme.liquid at top of <body>
Note: Please take the backup of the code before you paste the GTM script
Bigcommerce GTM Installation
Logging into your BigCommerce dashboard
Navigate to Advanced Settings
Select Web Analytics
Switch to the Google Analytics tab
Insert your GTM code in the text box
Click save
Go to https://tagmanager.google.com
Either create a new Google account or login to your existing account
Create a new GTM account and enter your account/business name, your country, and click continue
Name your Google Tag Manager container by putting the URL for your online store, choose “Web”, and click “Create”
Alternative Method

Alternatively, you can also insert the code directly into the template files within the <head></head> tags manually. This is likely the best way to ensure the code is accurately triggered for every page that loads on your site. You can do this by:
Logging into your BigCommerce dashboard
Navigate to Edit Theme Files > Templates > Layout > base.html
Paste the GTM code within the <head> tags, as high as possible.
Note: Please take the backup of the code before you paste the GTM script
Facebook Account Access Request
The
client/partner needs here is our business manager ID (865125153846998) and the basic instructions for how to share an ad account with another FB business manager.
Login to your business manager and go to business settings.
Select Users > Partners > Add > Give a partner access to your assets.
Copy and paste in our BM ID.
Select the assets they want to share with us, select "full control," and send.
Google Search Console process:
By connecting Google Search Console (GSC) with Google Ads, you get a snapshot glance of paid vs. organic keywords.
You will get data at the Search Query and Keyword levels to see the incremental value of paid vs. organic terms.
Login to >>
https://search.google.com/search-console/welcome?utm_source=about-page
Add Property >> Add the URL you want to submit in the search console
Under URL Prefix >> Add the link.
Once the code is added to the website, the dashboard will show the data. We have different kinds of verification methods. Uploading the html file directly on the website.
We will be able to get the data like search queries, top landing pages, clicks and CTR for each and every keyword.
2. Copy a small code on the website and paste on the website.
3. Verify through Google Analytics.
4. Verify through Google Tag manager
Verify with Domain Service provider
The below screenshot shows how it looks after verification is done.
The below screenshot shows the dashboard overview. We can see how many pages got indexes, search queries, CTR, and clicks for each keyword.
We can use the high CTR keywords
